plan: Create `package.json` with `dev`, `build`, and `preview` scripts using Vite, plus `lint`, `typecheck`, and `test` scripts (as required by CLAUDE.md testing requirements)
Create `tsconfig.json` with `strict: true` and appropriate Vite-compatible compiler options (target ESNext, module ESNext, moduleResolution bundler, jsx preserve)
Create `tsconfig.node.json` for Vite config file (Node-targeted tsconfig)
Create `index.html` at project root with a `<div id="app">` mount point and `<script type="module" src="/src/main.ts">`
Create `src/main.ts` with a minimal entry point that renders to the DOM without console errors
Create `src/vite-env.d.ts` with Vite client type reference (`/// <reference types="vite/client" />`)
Update `.gitignore` to include `node_modules/`, `dist/`, and other standard Vite ignores
Run `npm install` and verify it completes successfully
Run `npm run build` and verify production bundle is created in `dist/`
Run `npm run dev` briefly to verify dev server starts
Run `npm run preview` briefly to verify preview server starts
Open the default page in a browser context and confirm no console errors
testing_recommendations: Run `npm install` and confirm exit code 0 with no errors
Run `npm run dev` and confirm Vite dev server starts on a local port
Run `npm run build` and confirm `dist/` directory is created with `index.html` and JS assets
Run `npm run preview` and confirm the built bundle is served locally
Verify `package.json` contains exactly `dev`, `build`, and `preview` scripts (plus lint/typecheck/test stubs)
Open `tsconfig.json` and confirm `strict: true` is set
Load the default page in a browser and verify no console errors appear
Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to confirm TypeScript compiles cleanly with strict mode
risks: Vite/TypeScript version mismatches if not pinning versions — mitigate by using current stable versions
The `.gitignore` is minimal and may accidentally commit `node_modules/` or `dist/` if not updated early

Adding lint/typecheck/test scripts now (per CLAUDE.md) requires stub implementations or placeholder configs since ESLint and Vitest aren't in scope for this ticket — keep them as simple no-op or tsc-based commands
